message:
fix: add defensive undefined checks for SSR and data safety

This commit adds two related safeguards for undefined values:

1. IntersectionObserver SSR guard (use-intersection-observer.ts):
   Added early return when IntersectionObserver is undefined. This API
   doesn't exist during server-side rendering or in test environments
   like JSDOM. Without this check, attempting to instantiate
   new IntersectionObserver() would throw a ReferenceError on the
   server. The hook already defaults isIntersecting to true (to prevent
   flash of invisible content during SSR), so returning early maintains
   that behavior gracefully.

2. topStargazers null coalescing (notable-stargazers.tsx):
   The resolveRepoData function now uses ?? [] when accessing
   repoData.topStargazers and extracts it to a local variable before
   use. This prevents a runtime TypeError when calling .reduce() on
   undefined if a repo's topStargazers array is missing from the
   intelligence data. The extracted variable also ensures consistent
   handling between the assignment and the reduce calculation.

Both changes follow defensive programming principles for handling
potentially undefined values in data structures and browser APIs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
